desc: Taroudant is one of the strongest inland alternatives to Marrakech in southern Morocco: a walled market town with ochre ramparts, busy but not overwhelming souks, and enough everyday Amazigh and Souss Valley life to feel grounded rather than staged. It suits travellers who want medina atmosphere and day-trip range without the scale, hassle and tourism volume of Morocco's bigger names.

todo.name: Taroudant ramparts
todo.desc: Circle the ochre walls by caleche or on foot to appreciate the scale of the old trading city.

todo.name: Souk Arabe and Souk Berbere
todo.desc: Browse spices, leather and local crafts in a calmer setting than Marrakech.

todo.name: Tiout oasis trip
todo.desc: Head to the palm oasis and kasbah scenery for a classic Souss Valley day out.

todo.name: Anti-Atlas road loop
todo.desc: Use a driver for villages and dry-mountain landscapes when heat is manageable.

todo.name: Tannery and craft quarter wander
todo.desc: Look beyond the main souk lanes for leatherwork and quieter workshops that show Taroudant's more workaday side.

todo.name: Sunset wall walk or carriage loop
todo.desc: The ramparts make more sense late in the day, when the light softens and the medina starts to cool.

wifi.note: Usually fine in riads and central cafes, but once you move toward smaller guesthouses, mountain drives or oasis outings, coverage and consistency feel much less certain.

prac.fltNote: Taroudant is usually reached from Agadir rather than flown to directly, and ONDA's airport information for Agadir shows London Gatwick service at airport level. That makes Agadir the cleanest UK gateway, while Marrakech only makes sense if flight timing or a wider Morocco itinerary justifies the much longer inland transfer. In practice, heat, road timing and whether you plan mountain detours matter more here than airport choice alone.
prac.lang: Arabic and Amazigh; French is widely useful and English varies
prac.visa: British citizens can visit Morocco visa-free for up to 90 days for tourism, and GOV.UK says your passport should be valid for at least 3 months after arrival. Make sure your passport is stamped on entry, as missing stamps can cause problems when leaving.
alerts: Summer heat is intense inside the walls.
Friday and religious-holiday rhythms affect souk activity.
Taroudant is easier-going than Marrakech, but that also means quieter evenings and less polished tourist infrastructure.
Use guides or drivers for remote mountain routes.

sweet: April, May and October are usually the sweet spot for Taroudant, when the medina is still lively, the valleys are easier to explore and the heat has not yet flattened the day.
